messages.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this tunnel configuration?
messages.validation_errors: Please fix validation errors before saving
messages.confirm_start: Are you sure you want to start this tunnel?
messages.confirm_restart: Are you sure you want to restart this tunnel?
messages.confirm_stop: Are you sure you want to stop this tunnel?
messages.interactive_start_hint:
This tunnel requires interactive authentication. Please start it via terminal:
docker compose exec -it -u myuser autossh autossh-cli auth {hash}
messages.interactive_restart_hint:
This tunnel requires interactive authentication. Please stop it first, then start via terminal:
docker compose exec -it -u myuser autossh autossh-cli auth {hash}

validation.remote_port_format: Invalid port format. Use "port" or "hostname:port" (e.g., 44497 or lambda5:44497)
validation.local_port_format: Invalid port format. Use "port" or "ip:port" (e.g., 55001 or 192.168.1.100:55001)

tunnel_detail.title: Tunnel Details
tunnel_detail.name: Name
tunnel_detail.no_logs: No logs available yet. Start the tunnel to see logs.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: SSH Tunnel Manager is a Docker-based solution that uses autossh and YAML configuration files to manage SSH tunnels.

features.title: Key Features:
features.dockerized: Dockerized: Environment encapsulation, easy deployment and management
features.web_config: Web Configuration: Manage tunnel configuration through visual interface
features.auto_reload: Auto Reload: Detect configuration changes and automatically reload services
features.bidirectional: Bidirectional Tunnels: Support local-to-remote and remote-to-local tunnels
features.multi_arch: Multi-Architecture Support: Support AMD64, ARM64 and other architectures

interface.title: Interface Description:
interface.name: Name: Tunnel name for identification
interface.remote_host: Remote Host: Remote host address (e.g., user@server.com)
interface.remote_port: Remote Port: Remote port or host:port format
interface.local_port: Local Port: Local port or IP:port format
interface.direction: Direction: Tunnel direction (remote to local/local to remote)

interactive_auth.title: Interactive Authentication:
interactive_auth.description: Click the fingerprint icon to toggle interactive authentication status:

save_config.title: Save Configuration:
save_config.description: Click the Save & Restart button to save configuration and restart tunnel services.

tunnel_detail.title: Tunnel Detail Page:
tunnel_detail.description: Click on the status indicator in the main page table to open the tunnel's detail page.
tunnel_detail.features: The detail page provides the following features:
tunnel_detail.feature_control: Start, restart, or stop the tunnel

yaml_config.title: YAML Configuration
yaml_config.basic_comment1: Expose local service to remote server
yaml_config.basic_comment2: Map remote service to local port

ssh_config.title: SSH Configuration
ssh_config.important_desc: This project heavily relies on the ~/.ssh/config file for SSH connection configuration.

interactive_auth_cli.title: Interactive Auth CLI
interactive_auth_cli.description: For SSH servers that require two-factor authentication (2FA) or password authentication, you need to use the autossh-cli command-line tool for interactive authentication.

when_to_use.title: When to Use:
when_to_use.password: Server requires password authentication instead of key authentication

usage.title: Usage:
usage.intro: Follow these steps to start an interactive tunnel:
usage.step1: Enable interactive authentication option for the tunnel in the web interface
usage.step2: After saving the configuration, get the tunnel's hash value
usage.step3: Use the following command to start the interactive tunnel

hash_prefix.title: Hash Prefix Support:
hash_prefix.description: Similar to Git's short hash, you can use 8 or more characters of the hash prefix to identify tunnels.

other_commands.title: Other Common Commands:
other_commands.logs: View logs for a specific tunnel:

notes.title: Notes:
notes.tty: Must use -it parameter to enable interactive terminal
notes.user: Use -u myuser to specify the correct user identity
notes.foreground: Interactive tunnels run in the foreground and automatically move to background after successful authentication
notes.web_disabled: Tunnels with interactive authentication enabled cannot currently be started from the web interface; you must use the command line

advanced.title: Advanced Configuration
advanced.security_desc: When enabling remote port forwarding, you may need to configure GatewayPorts on the remote server:
